# All checklists {#sec-appendix-checklists}

Every checklist in the handbook, collected here as one printable tick sheet. Each links back to the chapter with the full context.

## Instructor setup {#sec-appendix-teacher}

From @sec-teacher-setup.

- [ ] Complete the full learner pre-course work yourself.
- [ ] Create a second "test learner" GitHub account with no special rights, kept in Chrome.
- [ ] Confirm owner rights on the iteration organization.
- [ ] Confirm Quarto is installed (`quarto --version`).
- [ ] Install `ghclass` and the R packages used in teaching.
- [ ] Book a room with projector, power, and reliable internet, and visit it before the day.
- [ ] Arrange the live-teaching equipment: flipchart/whiteboard and markers, a pointer, a second screen.
- [ ] Arrange catering or breaks for the four-hour session.
- [ ] Decide and communicate the participation model.

## Learner pre-course work {#sec-appendix-learner}

From @sec-learner-setup. Hand this to participants.

- [ ] Join the chat channel (Element / Matrix) and post a hello.
- [ ] Install the latest R from <https://cran.r-project.org>.
- [ ] Install the latest RStudio from <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/>.
- [ ] Create a GitHub account and verify your email.
- [ ] Generate a personal access token and save it safely.
- [ ] Install Git for your operating system from <https://git-scm.com>.
- [ ] Accept the cohort organization invitation and bookmark the org page.
- [ ] Clone the test repository in RStudio, commit a change, and push it.
- [ ] Confirm your change appears on GitHub.
- [ ] Read the "Why Git? Why GitHub?" introduction from *Happy Git and GitHub for the useR*.

## Create the iteration organization {#sec-appendix-orgs}

From @sec-create-orgs. The central `gitforsci` and development `gitforsci-dev` orgs already exist; you create only the iteration org.

- [ ] Create the `gitforsci-00x` iteration organization (in the browser).
- [ ] Confirm you are an owner; invite the test-learner account as a plain member.
- [ ] Generate `gitforsci-00x/website` from the `gitforsci-dev/website` template.
- [ ] Copy the exercise and assignment templates from `gitforsci-dev` into the iteration org with "Use this template."
- [ ] Invite learners and create their repositories with the `ghclass` scripts in `gitforsci-dev/admin`.
- [ ] Verify, as the test-learner, the org view, a clone, and a commit.
- [ ] Work the pre-course timeline backwards from the workshop date, leaving buffer after each due date.

## Build and publish the website {#sec-appendix-website}

From @sec-website.

- [ ] Generate `gitforsci-00x/website` from the template with "Use this template."
- [ ] Clone it in RStudio.
- [ ] Fill in every field in `_variables.yml`.
- [ ] Update cohort-specific fields in `_quarto.yml`.
- [ ] Render with `quarto preview` and check variables flow through.
- [ ] Commit, push, and publish (GitHub Pages or `quarto publish`).
- [ ] (Optional) Add Plausible analytics.
- [ ] Verify the published site as a logged-out test-learner.

## Before the day {#sec-appendix-before}

From @sec-before.

- [ ] Send pre-work with a due date a few days before.
- [ ] Open the pre-course survey (KoboToolbox) and collect GitHub usernames.
- [ ] Confirm room, projector, power, internet, equipment, catering.
- [ ] Invite learners to the iteration org with the `ghclass` scripts.
- [ ] Check who has accepted invitations and made a first commit; follow up with anyone who hasn't finished.
- [ ] Dry-run the whole workshop as the test-learner the day before.
- [ ] Morning of: confirm projector and internet, log in to both accounts, open site/slides/chat, set up the drawing surface and pointer.
- [ ] Have a triage plan for anyone who hasn't finished (pairing, direct follow-up).

## Run of show {#sec-appendix-runofshow}

From @sec-run-of-show.

- [ ] 0:00 Welcome and motivation; pre-work check.
- [ ] 0:30 Create and clone (Drawings 1 and 2).
- [ ] 1:15 Branch (Drawing 3).
- [ ] 2:00 Break.
- [ ] 2:15 Pull request and merge (Drawing 4).
- [ ] 3:15 Publish.
- [ ] 3:45 Wrap-up; name side benefits; point to follow-up.

## Spin up the next cohort {#sec-appendix-iteration}

From @sec-new-iteration.

- [ ] Update the templates in `gitforsci-dev` first.
- [ ] Create the new iteration organization.
- [ ] Generate and publish the cohort website.
- [ ] Copy material repositories into the new org.
- [ ] Sync the canonical `CITATION.cff` into the new repos.
- [ ] After the cohort runs, release the iteration website for its own DOI.
- [ ] Invite and verify learners with `ghclass`.
- [ ] Run the pre-flight checklist.

## Keep it open and citable {#sec-appendix-citation}

From @sec-citation-license.

- [ ] Add a `LICENSE` (CC-BY-4.0 for content, MIT for code).
- [ ] Maintain the canonical `CITATION.cff` in `gitforsci/website` with real ORCIDs.
- [ ] Mint the canonical course DOI via Zenodo from a GitHub release.
- [ ] Mint a per-iteration DOI from a release of each iteration org's website.
- [ ] Add the DOIs to the relevant `CITATION.cff` files and the landing page.
- [ ] Copy the canonical citation down into every other repository when it changes.
